title: Bridge Essay: Self-management and independence guidelines for the care of people with spina bi

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nondual/Unity** (Phase 16) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Nondual/Unity to mechanistic neuroscience through Self-management, and, independence, guidelines, for.

**Self-management and independence guidelines for the care of people with spina bifida.** — J Pediatr Rehabil Med. PMID: 33252094. DOI: 10.3233/PRM-200734. Self-management and independence behaviors are associated with improved health conditions common to spina bifida such as skin integrity and bowel and bladder management. While most children with spina bifida ultimately achieve basic self-care behaviors, (e.g., dressing appropriately, planning activities with peers, or cooking pre-planned meals), they often lag 2-5 years behind their typically-developing peers in these activities.
